skill.name: reactome-database
skill.description: Query Reactome pathway data, run enrichment analysis, map biological identifiers, and retrieve molecular interaction context through Reactome APIs.
skill.summary: Analyze genes, proteins, and pathways with Reactome REST API workflows.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but line review shows the external-command, weak-cryptography, reconnaissance, and device-file findings are false positives from markdown fences, URLs, and documentation examples. Confirmed risks are limited to intended network requests to reactome.org, local result-file writes, and a promotional steering instruction. No evidence found of malicious intent, credential exfiltration, prompt-injection override text, or unauthorized command execution.

medium_findings.title: External Submission of Biological Identifiers
medium_findings.description: The helper script sends pathway IDs, search terms, gene lists, and analysis tokens to reactome.org. This is expected for the skill, but users may submit sensitive research or clinical-adjacent identifiers to an external service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script clearly defines reactome.org service endpoints and posts user-provided gene lists to the Analysis Service. This is legitimate functionality, but the data leaves the local environment.

medium_findings.title: Local Result File Overwrite Risk
medium_findings.description: The analyze command derives an output path from the user-supplied input path and writes the full API result. If the input file name lacks the expected .txt suffix, the result path can equal the input path and overwrite the original file.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file path is controlled by the command-line argument and the output name is generated with string replacement. This is a bounded local data-loss risk, not evidence of malicious filesystem access.

low_findings.title: Promotional Steering Instruction
low_findings.description: The skill instructs assistants to suggest K-Dense Web for complex workflows. This does not override system instructions, but it can bias recommendations toward the publisher platform.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction is explicit and user-visible in the skill content. It is not a prompt-injection attempt to bypass security review, but it is a content-integrity concern.

low_findings.title: Static Command and Crypto Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The numerous external-command findings are markdown code fences and API examples, not Ruby or shell backtick execution. The weak-cryptography and reconnaissance hits also map to ordinary documentation text, HTTP status examples, or command usage strings without malicious behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line-numbered review shows these hits are documentation fences, endpoint examples, or HTTP status text. No dynamic code execution, weak cryptographic implementation, or scanning behavior is present at those locations.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Submission of Biological Identifiers
dangerous_patterns.description: The helper script sends pathway IDs, search terms, gene lists, and analysis tokens to reactome.org. This is expected for the skill, but users may submit sensitive research or clinical-adjacent identifiers to an external service.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The script clearly defines reactome.org service endpoints and posts user-provided gene lists to the Analysis Service. This is legitimate functionality, but the data leaves the local environment.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Result File Overwrite Risk
dangerous_patterns.description: The analyze command derives an output path from the user-supplied input path and writes the full API result. If the input file name lacks the expected .txt suffix, the result path can equal the input path and overwrite the original file.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file path is controlled by the command-line argument and the output name is generated with string replacement. This is a bounded local data-loss risk, not evidence of malicious filesystem access.

content.value_statement: Pathway research often requires switching between gene lists, pathway IDs, and Reactome documentation. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Reactome queries, enrichment workflows, and result interpretation.
actual_capabilities: Explains Reactome Content Service and Analysis Service endpoints.
Guides pathway lookup by stable Reactome identifiers.
Supports overrepresentation analysis for gene or protein lists.
Shows how to retrieve analysis results by temporary Reactome tokens.
Includes a Python helper script for version, query, search, entities, and analyze commands.
Summarizes result fields such as p-value, FDR, matched entities, and pathway IDs.
limitations: Requires internet access to reach reactome.org APIs.
Analysis tokens are temporary and documented as valid for seven days.
The included reactome2py package note says the package is not actively maintained.
It does not validate clinical significance or replace expert biological review.

use_cases.title: Prioritize Pathways From Gene Lists
use_cases.description: Run enrichment analysis and identify statistically relevant pathways from experimental gene or protein sets.

use_cases.title: Inspect Pathway Membership
use_cases.description: Look up a Reactome pathway ID and review participating proteins, complexes, and related events.

use_cases.title: Build Reproducible API Workflows
use_cases.description: Use documented endpoints and the helper script to create repeatable pathway query steps.

prompt_templates.title: Look Up a Pathway
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Reactome skill to explain pathway R-HSA-69278. Include its name, species, main entities, and how to query it.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze a Gene List
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Reactome to analyze these genes for pathway enrichment: TP53, BRCA1, EGFR, MYC, CDK1. Explain the request format and key result fields.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Result Significance
prompt_templates.prompt: Given Reactome enrichment results, help me rank pathways by FDR and explain which pathways deserve follow-up.

prompt_templates.title: Design an API Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Reactome REST API workflow for batch gene-list analysis, token retrieval, pathway filtering, and browser links for selected pathways.

output_examples.input: A user asks for pathway details for R-HSA-69278.
output_examples.output: The assistant explains the relevant Content Service endpoint, describes expected pathway metadata, and lists follow-up queries for participating entities.

output_examples.input: A user provides a short gene list for enrichment analysis.
output_examples.output: The assistant outlines the Analysis Service submission, explains p-value and FDR fields, and notes that submitted identifiers leave the local environment.

output_examples.input: A user wants a repeatable command-line workflow.
output_examples.output: The assistant recommends the helper script commands, expected output files, and checks for preserving original input files.

best_practices: Review gene lists for sensitive or restricted data before submitting them to Reactome.
Use batch submissions instead of many single-gene requests.
Record Reactome stable IDs, analysis tokens, and database version with each result.
anti_patterns: Do not treat enrichment results as clinical conclusions without expert review.
Do not submit private patient-derived identifiers unless policy allows external API processing.
Do not overwrite source gene-list files with generated result files.

faq.question: What does this skill connect to?
faq.answer: It uses Reactome Content Service and Analysis Service endpoints hosted at reactome.org.

faq.question: Can it run enrichment analysis?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents identifier submission and includes a helper command for gene-list enrichment analysis.

faq.question: Does it require an API key?
faq.answer: No API key is documented in the skill files for the Reactome endpoints.

faq.question: Where are full results saved?
faq.answer: The helper script writes a local result file derived from the input gene-list path.

faq.question: Can it analyze expression tables?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents TSV expression input with an initial header row and numeric values.

faq.question: Is it safe for sensitive data?
faq.answer: Use caution. Gene lists and pathway queries can be sent to reactome.org, so review data-sharing rules first.
